justification: All three required gaps identified: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly called out — mixed instances reading from different tables during rolling deploy. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 identified as a 'broken' interaction with no validation gate. (3) Non-resumable backfill explicitly flagged with recommendation for cursor/checkpoint. Specific recommendations match rubric: validation query (COUNT comparison + sampled integrity check), feature flag for atomic read-source switching, resumable backfill via cursor. Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Went beyond rubric requirements by identifying the backfill race condition where stale data overwrites fresh dual-written data — a real correctness bug not in the rubric but highly relevant
Feature flag recommendation is thorough — explains why rolling deploy alone is insufficient for coordinated cutover and why it's needed for instant rollback
Validation gate recommendation is concrete: COUNT(*) comparison plus sampled row-level data comparison as a hard prerequisite
Resumability recommendation is specific: cursor/checkpoint with idempotent restartable design
Comprehensive observability review identifies the debugging gap — on-call at 3am would be flying blind
Dual-write transactional guarantee gap is a valuable additional finding
Correctly avoided overcorrection — did not recommend halting the migration, instead provided specific fixes to make it safe
